title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I hope you are all enjoying lunch and the D&B Shipping Conference so far today, over the next 20 minutes or so, we'll try to shake things up a little bit and instead of getting the industry point of view, we'll discuss the outlook for shipping from the investor perspective.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: My name is Marius Brun Haugen, I work on the equity side here in D&B and also host our podcast Utbytte, where we will make the recording from this session available later on.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And to make this interesting for you guys, I'm joined here on stage by two investors that know shipping very well.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It is the Norwegian fund manager Hans Trane Nilsen from Storebrann and chief investment officer Willem.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And Will Holmen-Russell from VMC Capital, and give them a round of applause guys.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So Will, are you enjoying the conference so far?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Very much so, thank you.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That's good, you know, rumor has it that you are one of the few specialists that actually survived the last down cycle in shipping, so it's good to have you here.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Thank you, nice to be here.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And Hans, have you gotten any eye-opening input from the presentations or sessions you attended so far today?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Not quite.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I'm not particularly an eye-opener, but I'm amazed by the mood.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: There's that type of good mood here, people seem to be happy, and that's of course due to rates, but also I think a bit of optimism about the future.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So the mood is perhaps the most amazing part of the event so far.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Before we go into the discussion on the outlook for the shipping investments, I thought you could give us just a brief background on your fund and strategy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, Will, you go first.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, we run the Albemarle Shipping Fund, which is a pure shipping investment, long only.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Sorry, long short equity fund.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We set that up in January 2019.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Since then, we've returned about 320%, so just over 40% per annum.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And we're still very excited to continue investing in the shipping space and think there's a lot more to go.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yeah, you, Hans.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Norwegian long only equity funds.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Been doing that for in excess of 26 years.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I don't have an average annual return like you, but since inception,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: we've given around 1250% net return to our clients.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So it's been a good journey so far.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, Will, is your approach long term or short term when it comes to the investments and shipping?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And how do you rationalize it?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It is, I mean, well, we're permanently investing in the space, but it is really looking over the next 12, 18 months always.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Our average hold period is about 15 months of our current investments.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's very fundamentally driven.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So a lot of modeling of the underlying space of the companies we invest in.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: But we're always looking for the next subsector cycle to move into and out of.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Constant change, active management, but it's not kind of day to day trading.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's moving throughout the subsector cycles.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And Hans, how important would you say shipping is for your overall performance?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And how much time do you spend on the sectors?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Because I know you look at other sectors.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That's one part of our fund management process.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We have ten sectors in Norway which we can choose between.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And from time to time we do not own shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: For the last two years we've owned a lot of shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And this cycle seems to be lasting a bit longer than the average shipping cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Perhaps due to some regulation issues, but also lack of new buildings.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's very rare that we have a two, two and a half year up cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Almost without new buildings.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: In some segments.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And is it a paradox you think that what is, you know, normally considered among the most risky sectors,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: just go back two years ago, is now the place to be?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Because shipping is, right?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yeah, shipping stocks are more expensive than it was two years ago.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Two years ago, shipping stocks were quite very cheap.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Both absolute, but also relative in the Norwegian community.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And for all cyclical businesses, when there's no new buildings, when there's no new capacity,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: that tends to give a healthy rate of return in your income statements.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, when valuing shipping investments, what metrics do you tend to consider to time your entries and exits?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Is it all about pricing, Hans?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: No, I think there's more about the cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I mean, if you look at PEs in shipping, you won't find anything.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: If you look at P2 net as a down use, you might find something.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: But if you write about the length and the way the cycle goes,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: you can make huge amounts of money, perhaps no matter what the price of the company is at the start of the cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: What about you, Will?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We're always trying to look quite far ahead to what does it actually cost to build a ship in the future.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, the exciting part about this cycle, you're saying a couple of years ago everything looked very cheap.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We think we're in the bottom of a shipyard cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Which tends to last about 20 years, instead of the 5-7 years of a shipping cycle.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We think we've got quite a long way to go, because there's so little shipyard capacity,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and there's so much inflation there, we've run out of the deflationary forces we've had for 50 years

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: of ever cheaper new supply coming on in the Far East.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, we're looking at what does it cost in the future to build a ship, to replace a ship,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: what does that mean for rates of ships on the water, secondhand asset prices,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and we try to model that quite far ahead.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, you can say that the fundamentals is equally as important as the equity pricing?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Definitely.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Hans, you had a follow-up?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yeah, there are three interesting issues.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Of course, you should never say that this time is different.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Those are some of the world's most...

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Three issues which you need to take into account.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: One thing is the Japanese yards.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The Japanese have an older and older population, and they don't have access to labor anymore.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, Japanese yard capacity is diminishing.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: In Korea, the Qataris are building a huge amount of LNG ships.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That is occupying lots of yard capacity, especially at Daewoo and Samsung.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That makes less room for building conventional shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And third is China.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: From 1995 up until 2014-15, China increased yard capacity a lot.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Nowadays seems to be less capacity, no new capacity coming on stream in China.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, the three major shipholders

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: are acting counterintuitive to where the Sightner character is going.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That's good for shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: What about momentum?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Do you take this into consideration when investing?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The mood at this conference is a good indicator.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: If you can remember 2013, everyone here was very happy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: This is not the same, but it's approaching that level.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The amount of happiness is a point.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And what about track record?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: You know of the management, etc.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: How important is that for you guys when you invest?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It is very important.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's something you can't quantify so important that you can't qualify for it.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, there are a number of companies we favor because of the quality of management,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and some we stay away from because of the lack of quality.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: But yeah, it's more of a qualitative than quantitative factor.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I agree.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And dividends, I guess it's always there.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I mean, what can shipping companies currently generating a lot of cash do

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to maximize shareholder value in your view?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Would you like to start, Hans?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I don't think dividend itself is a very important thing

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: unless you have a good dividend policy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: You have to consider your balance sheets

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and stay away from too less and too much dividends.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And maybe the board should take into consideration the pricing of the share.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I know that's a difficult topic,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but some shipping companies tend to trade from time to time very cheaply.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Then you should definitely consider buybacks instead of dividends.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I know not all investors agree on that,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but I think it's possible to time your dividend policy

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: by having a steady dividend policy,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but from time to time,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: consider when to use buybacks or not.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: There's value to be gained there.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So Will, what's your take?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: How do one maximize shareholder value?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: No, I would agree with what you're saying.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's keeping that balance sheet sensible

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to keep the optionality there

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: so that you can enjoy the good markets when they're there,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but also keeping a good dividend policy,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the ability to buy back shares when they're cheap.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's being sensible.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I guess 15, 20 years ago,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: maybe shipping got a bad name

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: for being a little too exciting.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yeah.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's great to stand here to see them.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Some of the boards have to consider

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: whether they are overcapitalized.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Not every time you see that in shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yeah, yeah.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: You know, I had the pleasure of talking to

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Øystein Kalleklev earlier today at the conference.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: He's always a fun guy to follow on LinkedIn,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and last week he wrote the following.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We don't brag about that we are going to pay

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: a minimum dividend of X cents

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: or 50% of net profit.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So that management can enjoy themselves

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: with the remaining half the profits

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: pursuing empire building.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And he also said that, you know,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: if we come up to some good investment ideas,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: our job as management is to convince our shareholders

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to invest new money in such business opportunities

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: rather than having a pile of retained earnings

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to draw fund on for our tech projects.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Is it correct?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Øystein has for the last years

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: puttet his mind away from office

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and lived up to that policy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And I would also recommend everyone to follow

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Øystein Kalleklev on Twitter.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: He's fun to follow,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and he has some good comments there.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The group he's a part of,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: which is a decent group,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: has been very good at those topics

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: for nearly 20 years,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: or maybe even since he bought his first competitor,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: BNC companies in the late 90s.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So there are some issues there

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: very good at following and living up to.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And I guess we have touched upon it,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but we have to dig a little deeper.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So what would, from your perspective,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: will be the sort of general teams

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: that are making shipping

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: an interesting investment these days?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Sorry, what are the themes?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's very much this supply theme

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: that I've discussed already.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The fact that there's very little shipyard capacity,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: there's strong inflation at those shipyards,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the regulatory requirements mean

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the earlier phase out of ships.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's very much a supply chain

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: driven theme.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: At the same time,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: demand remains fairly healthy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We're moving into a phase of

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: what we believe will be a far more

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: capital and commodity intensive

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: part of the economy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The whole replacement of

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: all of our energy infrastructure

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: for green energy.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That's basically 20 years

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: of investment upfront.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: All of that is fantastic

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: for shipping of commodities

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: for steel, for cement, for oil.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: We have the ESG

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and all the regulation side of this.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: On one hand, you might argue,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: it's making things more complicated.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The ESG has got two significant factors

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: when it comes to influencing shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: One is the supply side.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: You don't as a board member know

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: what kind of fuel you need to use

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: or can use in the 2030s.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And when you order a ship today,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: it will live probably up until nearly 2050.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And when you don't know

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: what kind of machinery you can use,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: it's difficult.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: That limits

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the amount of supply we get.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The second are the EXI regulations,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: which probably will limit speed

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: of the fleet on the supply side.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Investors should look into

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: how large that effect will be.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: When regulation takes away supply,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: that's also good for shipping.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Keep a close look at that

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: in the various segments.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: The China reopening.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's been, I think,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: on everybody's mind this year.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: How does it look

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: from your view?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Will it help

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: some parts of the shipping,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: you think?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: Yes.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: I'm not fond of

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: praising regimes

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: with the policy like in China,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: but I must admit

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: like what they are doing.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: They are doing it well.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And the gradual opening,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: which we've seen so far

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: since late November

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: or maybe early December last year,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: is, I think, going according

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to the Communist Party's plan.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And that's good for shipping,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: especially hydrocarbons.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: And the Chinese are also smart.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: They are utilizing

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the war in Ukraine

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: by buying hydrocarbons

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: from central states,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: reloading it,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and sending it back

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to Europe and the West,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and making a profit

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: from that as well.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So China is playing

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: the current global political

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: environment quite well.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So, Will,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: does China seem to be bullish

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: on some parts of the shipping,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: or are there some signs

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: that things are a little bit softer

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: maybe than one should hope for?

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: No, I'd agree.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: It's kind of returned

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: to a more normal environment,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: and there's a lot of catch up there.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: There's a lot of transportation demands,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: which we're seeing

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: coming back very quickly.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: There is infrastructure stimulus coming.

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: So it's kind of more

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: returned to the norm

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
text: after many years of COVID,

title: Live podcast: Shipping Outlook from an Investor Perspective
